channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: WFI moves Supreme Court against Delhi High Court order permitting Vinesh Phogat in Asian Games selection trials
item.description: The Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) has approached the Supreme Court challenging a Delhi High Court order that allowed wrestler Vinesh Phogat to take part in the selection trials for the Asian Gam...

item.title: Tanzania Police Arrest Four in USD 20 Million Kidnapping and Extortion Case Involving Chinese Businessmen
item.description: Police in Dar es Salaam have arrested four Chinese nationals in connection with a serious kidnapping and extortion case involving two fellow Chinese businessmen. The suspects, identified as Fan Zhong ...

item.title: Man convicted of murdering university student after false racism claim led to victim’s wrongful arrest
item.description: A 23-year-old man, Vikrum Digwa, has been found guilty of murdering 18-year-old university student Henry Nowak following a fatal knife attack in Southampton. The court heard that Digwa used an eight-i...

item.title: Karnataka CM resignation, CBSE evaluation issues, and major legal and political developments across India
item.description: This Scroll “Rush Hour” roundup covers several significant political, administrative, and legal developments from across India. In Karnataka, senior Congress leader Siddaramaiah has resigned from the ...

item.title: Youth unemployment review warns of systemic failure and rising inactivity among young people in the UK
item.description: A government-commissioned review led by former Labour minister Alan Milburn highlights a deepening youth unemployment crisis in the UK, warning of a potential “lost generation” if urgent reforms are n...

item.title: Ontario proposes legislation allowing province to set policing priorities for local boards
item.description: The Ontario government has introduced legislation that would allow the province to set priorities for municipal police service boards, a move it says will improve consistency and clarity across polici...

item.title: NBI arrests two suspects involved in multiple derma clinic scam cases
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has successfully apprehended two individuals who were wanted in connection with multiple alleged fraudulent activities related to a dermatology clinic scam. ...

item.title: Government seeks to limit royal commission access to counter-terrorism documents amid public interest immunity claims
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s department has confirmed it has sought to restrict access to sensitive counter-terrorism material being examined by the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social ...

item.title: Astrophysical Calibration Improves Accuracy of Gravitational Wave Measurements
item.description: Since the first confirmed detection of gravitational waves in 2017, over 390 events have been recorded thanks to the combined efforts of LIGO, Virgo, and KAGRA detectors. The sensitivity of these dete...

item.title: Japan and Philippines to discuss intelligence-sharing cooperation, says Takaichi
item.description: The report indicates that Japan and the Philippines are set to discuss intelligence-sharing cooperation, with statements attributed to Takaichi. The coverage appears in a broader ABS-CBN article that ...

item.title: Stephanie O’Connor Explores Pregnancy and Microchimerism Through Composite Photography in The Waves Came in Like Horses
item.description: The LensCulture interview “The Waves Came in Like Horses” presents the photographic work of Stephanie O’Connor, a series that blends layered imagery to explore themes of pregnancy, identity, and bodil...

item.title: Labor pushes ahead with tax overhaul despite business backlash
item.description: The Australian government, led by Treasurer Jim Chalmers, has introduced a major tax reform bill to parliament aimed at reshaping capital gains tax (CGT), negative gearing, and delivering modest relie...

item.title: Steven Skybell reprises Tevye role in Yiddish Fiddler on the Roof at Toronto's Elgin Theatre
item.description: Stage veteran Steven Skybell is reprising his acclaimed role as Tevye in a Yiddish-language production of Fiddler on the Roof, which is being presented by the Harold Green Jewish Theatre Company at To...

item.title: CFL Announces Six-Year Broadcast Partnership with Bell Media, DAZN, and YouTube
item.description: The Canadian Football League (CFL) has announced a landmark six-year broadcast agreement starting in 2027 with Bell Media, DAZN, and YouTube, marking the league’s largest media valuation to date. Bell...

item.title: Lost 1979 Album by The Cramps Discovered and Released by Rollins and MacKaye
item.description: Henry Rollins and Ian MacKaye have revealed a previously lost studio album by the iconic psychobilly band The Cramps. The album, titled 'Gravest Gravy', originates from recording sessions in October 1...

item.title: Hotel reservation data exploited in targeted phishing scams affecting travelers worldwide
item.description: A new security investigation has revealed that customer booking data from more than 350 hotels, vacation rentals, motels, and guesthouses across 50 countries may have been accessed and weaponized in s...

item.title: EU fines Temu €200m for failing to prevent sale of illegal products
item.description: Chinese e-commerce platform Temu has been hit with a €200 million (approximately R3.8 billion) fine by European Union regulators for failing to properly prevent the sale of illegal products on its pla...

item.title: Hawthorn dominate St Kilda as Saints hit by injury blow and late fightback falls short
item.description: Hawthorn held a commanding lead over St Kilda at Marvel Stadium in their round 12 clash, with the Saints facing both a heavy scoreboard deficit and a concerning injury setback. The Hawks led 13.11 (89...

item.title: Kenya Kawadja: Falsified Phesgo batch flagged by Pharmacy Board amid patient-safety concerns
item.description: The Pharmacy and Poisons Board (PPB) in Kenya has issued a public alert about a counterfeit cancer medication circulating locally. The drug in question is Phesgo, a Roche-developed combination therapy...

item.title: AUATON urges Lagos government to regulate ride-hailing platforms over driver welfare and safety concerns
item.description: The Amalgamated Union of App-Based Transporters of Nigeria (AUATON) has formally petitioned the Lagos State Government, calling for urgent regulatory intervention in the operations of ride-hailing pla...
